channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Public university professors in Texas say a new law restricts their academic freedom
item.description: Public universities across Texas have instituted sweeping changes in recent months, from canceling gender studies programs to directing faculty to sign a pledge not to indoctrinate students.
item.content:encoded: Public universities across Texas have instituted sweeping changes in recent months, from canceling gender studies programs to directing faculty to sign a pledge not to indoctrinate students.
item.dc:creator: Camille Phillips

item.title: Colleges are trying to boost student voting. A Trump probe freezes data for that work
item.description: To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.
item.content:encoded:
To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: These blind students say their college blocked their education. A new rule could help
item.description: Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.
item.content:encoded:
Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: California teachers amend lesson plans after Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: Public school teachers across California have taught Cesar Chavez's contributions to the labor movement for a long time. Now they're figuring out how to revise those lessons given the recent allegations against him of sexual assault.
item.content:encoded: Public school teachers across California have taught Cesar Chavez's contributions to the labor movement for a long time. Now they're figuring out how to revise those lessons given the recent allegations against him of sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Katie DeBenedetti

item.title: How a SCOTUS decision on birthright citizenship could impact education access
item.description: All children, regardless of immigration status, have the right to a free K-12 public education. But without birthright citizenship, access to schools and colleges could get complicated.
item.content:encoded: All children, regardless of immigration status, have the right to a free K-12 public education. But without birthright citizenship, access to schools and colleges could get complicated.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: The depleted Education Department will move out of its headquarters
item.description: In August, Education Department employees will relocate to a smaller office roughly a block away, and the larger Energy Department will take over the old headquarters.
item.content:encoded:
In August, Education Department employees will relocate to a smaller office roughly a block away, and the larger Energy Department will take over the old headquarters.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Federal student loans will move to Treasury, further shrinking Education Department
item.description: The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Cursive is back. But should students be learning the skill?
item.description: A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?
item.content:encoded:
A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?

(Image credit: Anna Rose Layden for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.content:encoded: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman
